_note: Tooling-layer demo with TWO MCP servers. (1) 'example' — the official MCP reference server (@modelcontextprotocol/server-everything), stdio over npx, NO auth: it connects out of the box, so `/mcp` visibly shows a live MCP with example tools (echo, get-env, get-sum, …). It is a placeholder to demonstrate the tooling layer — swap it for your real servers. Pre-warm with `npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything` before a live demo so the first connect isn't a download. (2) 'datadog' — a realistic example (official Datadog MCP, HTTP + OAuth). The endpoint is org/site-specific: set DATADOG_MCP_URL via `/ddsetup` or your site's docs. With it UNSET, Claude Code cannot resolve the empty ${DATADOG_MCP_URL} and the server silently fails to connect — it will NOT appear in `/mcp`. That is expected: it is a needs-setup placeholder. GitLab and AWS are deliberately NOT MCP servers — their CLIs (glab, aws) cover the ground and will become skills later. No secret is ever committed here.
